digest.component_or_area: identity entity + authorship certification + provider-neutral user mapping (Jira adapter)
propositions: rebar's author field is an unauthenticated free string (git config user.name) that anyone with push access can forge, so events cannot be reliably attributed to a real person.

Model identity as a first-class event-sourced entity with a stable opaque id storing name, email, {provider,external_id} mappings, and public keys only, with a write-time guard rejecting private-key material.

Add an optional authorship attestation (DSSE envelope wrapping an in-toto Statement binding {event_uuid, content_hash}) signed via the foundation substrate and verified offline against the identity's in-band keys; an unavailable key yields an unsigned Tier-0 write that never blocks.

Establish key validity via TOFU self-signed genesis plus G2 (add/rotate/revoke signed by a currently-valid key), with era scoped by tickets-branch commit ancestry refined by intra_idx (added_at/revoked_at positions).

Provide a provider-neutral local↔accountId mapping keyed on the provider's opaque id so the Jira reconciler sets assignee (and reporter when Modify-Reporter is held), degrading gracefully via a fallback chain rather than hard-failing.

Enforcement is opt-in via a CI merge-gate running verify-identity --require-authenticated --since enforce_since over the merged log (detect-and-fail, not pre-receive reject), grandfathering pre-cutover events while replay never rejects and default install stays byte-identical with no auto-minted identity.
